channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil Climbs Above $100 as U.S. Plans Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The U.S. said it would block ships entering or exiting Iranian ports or coastal areas starting at 10 a.m. Eastern on Monday. It was unclear how that would affect the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman’s Musandam province on Sunday.

item.title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that, if only so much.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Celebrations in Budapest on Sunday after election results started coming in.

item.title: In Myanmar, Even Flowers Stoke Fear
item.description: Myanmar’s generals have brutalized the country for decades. But they have come to dread garlands as a sign of resistance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.media:description: The bombs come at any time. The only constant is that they will.

item.title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
item.description: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in November in Istanbul. On that visit, the pope engaged in interfaith dialogue, and similar discussions may come up during his Africa trip.

item.title: ‘The Strangest Sleepover Ever’: Dutch Skeptical as Their Royals Visit Trump
item.description: The Dutch king and queen are set to stay at the White House on Monday night. At a time of trans-Atlantic tension, the Netherlands isn’t sure how it feels about that.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert and Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands are headed to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Aiming at China, Malaysia Puts New Restrictions on Electric Cars
item.description: The move was a tacit acknowledgment of China’s dominance in the global market and the power of its automakers to keep prices low.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed
item.media:description: An electric car made by Perodua, a Malaysian automaker, at a launch event in Kuala Lumpur in December.

item.title: After Chaotic Election Day, Peru Orders 2nd Day of Voting for Some Areas
item.description: Frustrated voters in long lines were unable to cast ballots in parts of the capital, Lima, due to logistical failures, eroding trust for some in the process.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Mitra Taj
item.media:credit: Sara Wayra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A presidential campaign event for Rafael López Aliaga, leader of the Popular Renewal Party, in Lima last Thursday.

item.title: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Sunday. His defeat will please the European Union and is likely to disappoint President Trump.

item.title: Who Is Peter Magyar, the Man Who Toppled Hungary’s Orban
item.description: Mr. Magyar’s success in the Hungarian election is fueled in large part by widespread public anger at corruption and concern about sluggish economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar at a rally last month for his party, Tisza, in Keszthely, Hungary. He has promised to improve relations with the European Union.

item.title: In Pakistan Talks, Iran Saw a U.S. Trying to Dictate, Not Negotiate
item.description: Iran sees American demands as reaching far beyond what the United States achieved in war. Tehran is gambling that it can withstand further bombardment more than Washington is willing to sustain economic chaos, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, on Saturday in Tehran. Iranian officials argue that the United States did not actually want to negotiate during talks this weekend in Pakistan.

item.title: Cuba’s President Projects Defiance in Standoff With the U.S.
item.description: Miguel Díaz-Canel’s stance on “Meet the Press” reflected how Cuba is digging in against pressure from a Trump administration distracted by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Adam B. Ellick and Simon Romero
item.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba in Havana last month. He told “Meet the Press” on Sunday, “We’ll defend ourselves, and if we need to die, we’ll die.”

item.title: The ‘Islamabad Peace Talks’ Are Over. What Now for Pakistan?
item.description: With the U.S. and Iranian delegations gone from Islamabad and the cease-fire uncertain, Pakistan faces its own economic and diplomatic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, right, with Vice President JD Vance, ahead of the peace talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Saturday.

item.title: Mass Stampede at Haitian Tourist Site Leaves Dozens Dead
item.description: The tragedy unfolded around the appearance of a social media personality at the Citadelle Laferrière, a fortress that is a symbol of Haitian sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre, Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Simon Romero
item.media:description: The Citadelle Laferrière, a large 19th-century fortress in northern Haiti, in 2024.

item.title: Who Is Viktor Orban?
item.description: Hungary’s prime minister, once a liberal activist, has governed for the past 16 years as a champion of “illiberal democracy.”
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has throttled Hungary’s news media, cracked down on migrants and fought against progressive causes.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers, Tyler Pager, Aaron Boxerman and Isabel Kershner

item.title: In the Desert, a ‘Cleaning Station’ for Ants
item.description: A unique “choreography” between two ant species suggests a distinctive partnership in which one provides a carwash service to the other.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil

item.title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s Voyage to Africa
item.description: The pontiff will visit four countries on the world’s poorest continent, demonstrating his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican last month. His 10-day trip to Africa begins on Monday and includes visits to Algeria, Angola, Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea.

item.title: China’s Electrostate Is Poised to Win From War in the Middle East
item.description: A cluster of Chinese firms is poised to win big from the energy crisis set off by the war in the Middle East, building on booming investment in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
item.media:description: An electricity transmission hub is at the terminus in south-central China of the world’s longest and most powerful ultrahigh-voltage power line. Chinese companies dominate the manufacturing of nearly every piece of a modern grid.

item.title: How Would a Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Work? Here Are Some Possibilities.
item.description: The goal of the naval operation ordered by President Trump is to deny Iran the revenues it needs to fund its military, but there are many unknowns.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Scores Killed in Nigerian Military Strikes as Clashes With Militants Intensify
item.description: Nigerian officials said they attacked a terrorist enclave, but locals and rights groups said the airstrikes hit a popular market, killing many civilians.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Eric Schmitt

item.title: An Underwater Phantom Is Killing Fish and Breaking Hearts
item.description: A mysterious algal bloom has haunted South Australia’s coast for more than a year, killing marine life and unsettling communities that cherish the ocean.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: Christine Fréchette Is Chosen to Lead Coalition Avenir Québec
item.description: Far behind in the polls, Quebec’s governing party chose Christine Fréchette to succeed François Legault and to lead it in a vote this fall.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: Christine Fréchette, in Drummondville, Quebec, on Sunday.

item.title: Israel’s War
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was instrumental in taking the U.S. to war with Iran. Now, he is trying to prolong the fighting.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s Underground Air Force Base Was Hit Earlier in the War
item.description: Satellite imagery shows impact craters at and near the tunnel entrances to aircraft shelters at Iran’s remote “Eagle 44” base.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Says Talks Failed Because U.S. Failed to Win Trust
item.description: Iranian officials said that while Iran had approached the negotiations in good faith, the U.S. team had not gained the Iranian delegation’s trust.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, Iran’s top negotiator and the speaker of its Parliament, said the U.S. team had failed to “gain the trust of the Iranian delegation.”

item.title: Asha Bhosle, One of India’s Most Beloved Singers, Dies at 92
item.description: Her distinctive and adventurous voice was the soundtrack for generations of Bollywood films.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Rahav Segev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Asha Bhosle was cited by the Guinness World Records for the most studio song recordings by any artist.

item.title: Clearing naval mines is painstaking, dangerous work.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: How JD Vance Tried and Failed to End the War in Iran That He Opposed
item.description: Vice President JD Vance led the highest-level talks between the United States and Iran in nearly 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President Vance arriving for a news conference after meeting with representatives from Pakistan and Iran in Islamabad on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Launches New Attacks in Lebanon, Days Ahead of Rare Direct Talks
item.description: Israel’s campaign targeting Hezbollah in Lebanon has been a source of tension in the U.S.-Iran cease-fire. Israeli and Lebanese officials plan to meet for rare talks in Washington this week.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A building on Saturday in the Lebanese capital, Beirut, that was destroyed by Israeli strikes.

item.title: Netanyahu Says War With Iran Is ‘Not Yet Over’
item.description: In a televised address, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel sought to placate critics at home who say the war failed to achieve its goals.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem last month.

item.title: Peru Votes for President, With 35 Names on the Ballot
item.description: Few expect the vote to resolve the political instability that has plagued the country for years. Three presidents have come and gone since 2021.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky
item.media:credit: Sara Wayra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A campaign event for Rafael López Aliaga, leader of the Popular Renewal Party, in Lima, Peru, on Thursday.

item.title: Inside Tyre, a Lebanese City Bombarded by Israel
item.description: In Tyre, a city on Lebanon’s coast, near-daily bombardments by Israel have killed and injured civilians, and left many searching for shelter.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lebanese medics transport a man critically wounded by an Israeli strike in the back of an ambulance outisde a hospital in Lebanon’s southern port city of Tyre, last month.

item.title: The Escalating Global A.I. Arms Race
item.description: China, the U.S., Russia and others have ramped up their contest over artificial-intelligence-backed weapons and military systems. The buildup has been compared to the dawn of the nuclear weapons age.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel, Paul Mozur and Adam Satariano
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kristian Thacker for The New York Times
item.media:description: Anduril’s autonomous air vehicle, Fury, which recently began production at the company’s new factory outside Columbus, Ohio.

item.title: Rejected by Dozens of Emergency Rooms: South Korea’s Medical Crisis
item.description: Hundreds of patients every year have trouble finding emergency care, even though the country has a world-class medical system. For some, the delays have been fatal.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers at a hospital in Seongnam, a city outside Seoul.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers, Tyler Pager, Aaron Boxerman and Isabel Kershner

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s Uranium Stockpiles Were Sticking Points in U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: The U.S. had demanded that Iran immediately reopen the strait to all maritime traffic, but Iran said it would do so only after a final peace deal, according to Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Journalists broadcasting on Saturday from the Jinnah Convention Center in Islamabad, Pakistan, near where Iranian and U.S. officials were meeting.

item.title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
item.description: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance leaving after negotiations with Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday.

item.title: What to Know About the Hungary Elections as Orban Fights to Remain in Power
item.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a standard-bearer for global populism, in political peril, the vote on Sunday could have far-reaching implications.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:description: Viktor Orban, Hungary’s prime minister of 16 years, campaigning in Szekesfehervar on Friday.

item.title: Many Polls Say Orban Will Lose. But He Has an Edge Even Before Voting Begins.
item.description: Over 16 years, Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party has repeatedly tweaked the electoral system to its advantage, making the vote free, but not entirely fair.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary addressing his supporters during an election rally on Friday.

item.title: Jailed for Love Songs? Yes, and Still Singing.
item.description: Nguyen Van Loc was persecuted for performing Hanoi’s old romantic music during (and after) the Vietnam War. He can’t stop. He won’t stop.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave and Linh Pham
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Moves to Deport Son of Prominent Figure in Iranian Revolution
item.description: Federal agents arrested a man whose mother served as a spokeswoman for the Islamist embassy captors during the hostage crisis that began in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Trump administration announced plans to deport the son of Masoumeh Ebtekar, the former vice president of Iran, seen here during a news conference in 2019.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Talks Timeline: Key Moments Between Two Adversaries
item.description: From sanctions relief to nuclear talks, here’s a look at how negotiations between Washington and Tehran have gone over the decades.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, on Friday as the city prepared to host the talks between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Overlooked No More: Margaret Gipsy Moth, Fearless CNN Camera Operator
item.description: She covered conflicts across the globe, joking after she was injured in Sarajevo that she would be returning to find her missing teeth.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Schachar

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows China Taking a More Active Role in Iran War
item.description: China may have shipped missiles to Iran, and Beijing is allowing some companies to sell Tehran supplies that can be used in military production, American officials said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An anti-U.S. billboard on Sunday in Tehran showing American aircraft captured in a net.

item.title: U.K. Puts Chagos Islands Deal on Hold Following Criticism From Trump
item.description: The plan to give Mauritius formal control of the islands, home to a U.S.-British military base, was called “an act of great stupidity” by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: An image of Diego Garcia, the largest of the Chagos Islands, released by the United States Navy. The islands are home to a key U.S.-British military base.

item.title: Monthly Report
item.description: What can we learn from April, a month of contradictions that never cleanly resolve themselves?
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: When the Moon Met Canada
item.description: Canada’s technical expertise in space robotics got the country a seat to the moon, and then its cultural identity took center stage on the Artemis II mission.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Bill Ingalls/Agence France-Presse, via NASA/AFP via Getty Images
item.media:description: Jeremy Hansen of the Canadian Space Agency being escorted aboard the U.S.S. John P. Murtha on Friday after returning from a trip to the moon.

item.title: There are few public details about the high-level talks — not even the timing.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance being greeted on Saturday by Pakistani officials in Islamabad, Pakistan.

item.title: Many Iranians Are Glad the Fighting Has Paused. Some Hard-liners Aren’t.
item.description: The most fervent backers of the Islamic republic believe pausing the war for negotiations risks squandering what they see as a hard-won upper hand.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Iran Tries to Grasp Economic Devastation of War, and Find a Way Past It
item.description: The vast scale of destruction wrought by U.S. and Israeli bombardment will make sanctions relief all the more vital to Iran’s government as it tries to negotiate a peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Assessing the aftermath of a U.S.-Israeli airstrike this month on a bridge in Karaj, Iran.

item.title: Killing of Masood Masjoody Exposes Deep Rifts in Canada’s Iranian Diaspora
item.description: An Iranian activist in Vancouver disappeared after accusing two compatriots of wanting him dead. Then his body was found.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Sarah Berman
item.media:credit: Alana Paterson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A dance workshop last month in Vancouver, British Columbia, to celebrate Nowruz, the Persian New Year. Despite a shared cultural heritage, political divisions within the Iranian diaspora are deep, and disagreements often bitter.

item.title: Mexico’s Police Focus on World Cup While Thousands Remain Missing
item.description: Mexico’s heavy security investment for the World Cup is drawing criticism from families of the disappeared, who argue the focus on safety for teams and fans ignores their search for missing loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Brent McDonald, Souleyman Messalti, Miguel Tovar, Ben Laffin and Mark Boyer
item.media:description: Ana Hatsumi Muñoz, a member of the Guerreros Buscadores search collective, stands before an altar for four of her family members that have disappeared or been murdered in recent years.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi, Elian Peltier and Aaron Boxerman

item.title: Iran Looks to Project Unity With Large Delegation to Peace Talks
item.description: At least 70 people are in the team that is scheduled to negotiate with the American side in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters
item.media:description: A photograph released by Pakistan’s government showing the Iranian delegation led by the speaker of Iran’s parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, second from right; and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, second from left. They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Growing List of Orban Loyalists Defecting Before Critical Election
item.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban seeming vulnerable before Sunday’s vote, criticism is growing from within institutions his party once counted on for support.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Zalan Alkonyi in Budapest, Hungary’s capital. As a researcher focused on Russia, he says he has come under pressure to toe the government line.

item.title: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
item.description: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators burning American and Israeli flags on Wednesday in Tehran after a cease-fire was announced.

item.title: How Lu Xun, a Famous Chinese Writer, Became a Cute Communist Mascot
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party has turned Lu Xun, a Mao-era hero who excoriated the establishment, into a bland, Disney-style character.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: In New War With Israel, Hezbollah Defies Notion That It Was Crippled
item.description: The Iran-backed Lebanese militant group surprised many with the intensity of its attacks on Israel in the current conflict.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Israeli soldier at the site of a Hezbollah missile strike in Haniel, Israel, last month.

item.title: Fallout of War Piles Economic Pain Onto Europe’s Political Stress
item.description: Europe is finding itself on the outs with Russia, China and the U.S., in what’s amounting to its very own “Mean Girls” moment.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick Junker for The New York Times
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas terminal in Germany. Most of that L.N.G. comes from the United States, underscoring Europe’s vulnerability to American supplies.

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.
